title: Navarretia minutiflora
taxonomy: Navarretia minutiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, commonly known as the phlox family. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the genus Navarretia, which is a group of annual or perennial herbaceous plants. The genus Navarretia is further classified under the order Ericales, which is a diverse group of flowering plants that also includes families such as the heath and the blueberry. Navarretia minutiflora is a member of the class Magnoliopsida, which is the largest class of flowering plants.

The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues.
